rows.query: How do I reset my password?
rows.answer:
Quick steps — if you can’t log in
- Go to the company login page and click “Forgot Password?”; enter the email for your account and Submit. Check your inbox (or spam) for the “Password Reset Request” email and click the reset link. Create a new password that meets complexity rules (≥8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special char) and submit, then log in with the new password. [How to reset your login password]

If you use Single Sign‑On (SSO)
- Use the password reset portal at passwordreset.companyname.com, enter your SSO email, follow the emailed reset link, set a new password (same complexity requirements), then sign in at sso.companyname.com. If you still have problems, contact the IT Help Desk at helpdesk@companyname.com or ext. 1234. [Password reset for SSO users]

If your password is expiring (or you can still log in)
- You can change it from Account Settings → Change Password by entering your current password and a new one that meets the complexity rules. You’ll receive expiry reminders (30, 15, 7, 1 days before expiry). [Understanding password expiry notifications]

Sources:
- How to reset your login password (3e035404-1689-5439-a402-f65b70c1d0bd)
- Password reset for SSO users (b68e7598-88b5-540c-a7b3-0cb1c335111a)
- Understanding password expiry notifications (1adab1e1-efe2-5dc8-921e-11d293ceaab4)

Would you like me to send a password-reset email link for your account or open a ticket with IT?
rows.faithfulness_justification: All procedural details match the sources, but the offer to directly send a reset link or open a ticket is not supported by the provided excerpts.
rows.helpfulness_justification: It gives clear, step-by-step instructions for standard, SSO, and expiring-password scenarios with contact info, enabling the user to complete the reset.

rows.query: I lost my phone with the authenticator app on it — how do I get back into my account?
rows.answer:
First try these steps (from the knowledge base) to regain access:

- Use your backup codes: when you set up the authenticator you should have saved backup codes — enter one at the MFA prompt to sign in. [Recovering MFA when you lose your phone]  
- Try alternate MFA methods: look for an option like “use alternative method” (email, SMS, or security questions) on the MFA prompt and follow the on-screen steps. [Recovering MFA when you lose your phone]  
- If you don’t have backup codes or alternate methods, contact IT support and submit a ticket. Include your full name, employee ID, a description of the issue, and any backup codes you tried. IT will verify your identity and reset your MFA. [Recovering MFA when you lose your phone]  
- After IT resets MFA, reconfigure a new authenticator (scan the QR or enter the setup key) and save the new backup codes in a secure place. [Recovering MFA when you lose your phone]  
- As a precaution, change your account password, review recent account activity for anything suspicious, and report the lost phone to your carrier (consider remote wipe if available). [Recovering MFA when you lose your phone]

Would you like me to open a support ticket for you now? If so, please confirm and provide the full name and employee ID to include in the request.

Sources:
Recovering MFA when you lose your phone (5d66533c-d8d2-58df-aaaf-8685a82fb7d5)
rows.faithfulness_justification: All steps align with the recovery article except for citing SMS as an alternate MFA method, which the source lists as email or security questions, making that a minor unsupported embellishment.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Clear, actionable steps cover all recovery paths (backup codes, alternate methods, IT reset, re-setup, securing account) and offers to open a support ticket, fully enabling the user to regain access.

rows.query: Walk me through setting up the VPN on my Mac.
rows.answer:
Follow these steps from the knowledge base to set up a VPN on macOS [VPN setup guide (macOS)]:

1. Prerequisites
   - Get your VPN server address, username, password, and any shared secret or certificate from IT.

2. Open Network preferences
   - Apple menu → System Preferences → Network.

3. Add a VPN connection
   - Click the + button (bottom left).
   - Interface: select VPN.
   - VPN Type: choose the type your company uses (L2TP, IKEv2, etc.).
   - Service Name: give it a name (e.g., "Company VPN") → Create.

4. Configure VPN settings
   - Enter Server Address and Account Name.
   - Click Authentication Settings and enter password and shared secret or select a certificate as required → OK.

5. Advanced options (optional)
   - Click Advanced to enable "Send all traffic over VPN" or add DNS servers if IT instructed.

6. Apply and connect
   - Select the new VPN in the left list → Apply → Connect.
   - Status should change to Connected after a few moments.

7. Verify & disconnect
   - Verify by checking your IP (e.g., whatismyip.com).
   - To disconnect: go back to Network preferences, select the VPN, and click Disconnect.

Troubleshooting
   - Re-check server address, username, and password.
   - Ensure your internet connection is active.
   - Contact IT helpdesk if problems persist.

Sources:
- VPN setup guide (macOS) (691e9509-032b-571a-9109-2922b2f3c14c)
rows.faithfulness_justification: All steps and details closely match the macOS VPN setup guide in the sources, with no unsupported claims.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Provides a complete, step-by-step walkthrough with prerequisites, configuration, verification, disconnect, and troubleshooting tailored to macOS.

rows.query: What is the software reimbursement policy?
rows.answer:
Key points from the Software Reimbursement Policy [Software Reimbursement Policy]:

- Eligibility
  - Must be directly related to your job, not available through existing company licenses, and purchased within the current fiscal year. [Software Reimbursement Policy]

- Cost limits and approvals
  - Purchases up to $500 may be reimbursed without prior approval.
  - Purchases over $500 require prior written approval from your department head. [Software Reimbursement Policy]

- How to request reimbursement
  1. Purchase the software with a personal payment method and keep the receipt.  
  2. Complete the company reimbursement form (available on the intranet) including required fields (name, ID, dept, purchase date, software name, business justification, amount).  
  3. Attach the receipt and any supporting documentation.  
  4. Submit the form for manager approval (via the designated process).  
  5. If approved, the request goes to finance and approved reimbursements are processed in the next payroll cycle. [Software Reimbursement Policy]

- Timeframe and compliance
  - Requests must be submitted within 30 days of purchase.  
  - Software must comply with company security and licensing policies. [Software Reimbursement Policy]

- Contacts
  - For questions, contact IT Support or HR (contact details on the article). [Software Reimbursement Policy]

Sources:
Software Reimbursement Policy (29c57aef-0e3b-5b40-8600-f7ec00c07cdd)

Would you like the reimbursement form link or help preparing a submission?
rows.faithfulness_justification: Accurately summarizes eligibility, steps, timing, compliance, contacts, and >$500 approval; slightly infers that ≤$500 requires no prior approval without being explicitly stated.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Directly answers with clear, actionable summary and steps that fully enable the user to follow the policy.

rows.query: What's new in BlueDesk v5.2 compared to v5.1?
rows.answer:
I checked the release notes for both versions. Summary of what’s new in BlueDesk v5.2 vs v5.1:

- Reporting: v5.2 adds customizable report templates and new visualization types (pie charts, line graphs). v5.1 introduced a comprehensive reporting module with multi-format export (PDF/Excel/CSV) [BlueDesk v5.2 release notes] [BlueDesk v5.1 release notes].  
- API: v5.2 introduces BlueDesk API v2.0 with new endpoints for third‑party integrations and adds OAuth 2.0 support for API access. v5.1 added support for specific third‑party integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot) and improved API documentation [BlueDesk v5.2 release notes] [BlueDesk v5.1 release notes].  
- Performance and resource usage: both note a ~30% improvement in data retrieval from optimized DB queries. v5.1 explicitly reported a 15% reduction in background memory usage; v5.2 also states reduced memory usage in background services but does not give a percent [BlueDesk v5.2 release notes] [BlueDesk v5.1 release notes].  
- UI/accessibility: both releases list dashboard/UI improvements and accessibility updates (ADA) — no major UI feature listed as unique to v5.2 in the notes [BlueDesk v5.2 release notes] [BlueDesk v5.1 release notes].  
- Bug fixes / stability: v5.2 specifically fixes slow loading on the analytics page and synchronization problems with external data sources. v5.1 focused on resolving intermittent login failures, notification delays, and data sync errors [BlueDesk v5.2 release notes] [BlueDesk v5.1 release notes].  
- Upgrade guidance and known issues: v5.2 includes upgrade steps and lists minor known UI glitches in some browsers and potential third‑party compatibility updates; v5.1 noted API rate limiting during peak hours and some mobile UI compatibility issues [BlueDesk v5.2 release notes] [BlueDesk v5.1 release notes].

Sources:
- BlueDesk v5.2 release notes (2b66711b-9535-5c42-b9cf-01694607024d)  
- BlueDesk v5.1 release notes (58d96b3b-de9a-5e9f-aa29-b8ba1d17d76e)

If you want, I can provide the exact upgrade steps from the v5.2 notes or check whether any of your installed third‑party integrations need updates.
rows.faithfulness_justification: All summarized differences (reporting, API, performance, UI/accessibility, bug fixes, upgrade notes, known issues) are directly supported by the provided v5.1 and v5.2 release notes.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Clear, concise comparison of v5.2 vs v5.1 across key areas fully answers the question and is immediately actionable for understanding what’s new.

rows.query: How do I report a phishing email I received?
rows.answer:
Follow these steps:

1. Do NOT click links or open attachments in the suspicious message.  
2. Look for phishing signs (bad grammar, urgent requests, unfamiliar sender, generic greeting).  
3. Take a screenshot that shows the email header and body.  
4. Forward the email to the IT security team at security@yourcompany.com. In the forward, say why you think it’s phishing and note any actions you already took (e.g., clicked a link).  
5. After forwarding, delete the message from your mailbox.  
6. If you clicked a link or opened an attachment, run a full antivirus scan immediately and follow the scan’s recommendations.  
7. If you gave away credentials or other sensitive info, change your password right away.  
8. For severe cases (financial loss or identity theft), consider reporting to external authorities such as the FTC.  
9. If you’re unsure or need help, contact the IT security team.

[Reporting a phishing email]

Sources: Reporting a phishing email (4603725b-3299-57dd-8c1a-257052964a02)
rows.faithfulness_justification: All steps and details in the answer match the source guidance (forward to security@yourcompany.com, include rationale and actions taken, screenshot, delete, AV scan, password change if info given, external reporting like FTC, contact IT).
rows.helpfulness_justification: Direct, step-by-step instructions clearly tell the user exactly how to report the phishing email and what follow-up actions to take if they interacted with it.

rows.query: How do I set an out of office reply?
rows.answer:
Which email client do you use? Meanwhile, here are quick steps for common clients:

- Outlook (desktop): File → Automatic Replies (Out of Office) → select "Send automatic replies" (optionally set a time range) → enter internal and external messages → OK. [Setting an out-of-office auto-reply]

- Outlook (web): Sign in → Settings (gear) → View all Outlook settings → Mail → Automatic replies → toggle on, set time range if needed, compose internal/external messages → Save. [Setting an out-of-office auto-reply]

- Gmail: Sign in → Settings (gear) → See all settings → General tab → Out of Office AutoReply → turn on, set first/last day and message (option to send only to contacts) → Save Changes. [Setting an out-of-office auto-reply]

Remember to turn the auto-reply off when you return.  

Sources: Setting an out-of-office auto-reply (06d19721-5376-54d0-a2e7-80112108a985)
rows.faithfulness_justification: All steps and notes match the provided out-of-office setup instructions for Outlook (desktop/web) and Gmail, including the reminder to turn it off.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Direct, actionable steps for major clients answer the question immediately, with a prompt to specify the client if different.

rows.query: How do I add a network printer?
rows.answer:
Follow these steps (Windows) to add a network printer:

Prerequisites
- Make sure the printer is powered on and connected to the network, and get its IP address. Have admin access on your workstation. [Adding a network printer]

Steps
1. Open Control Panel (Windows+R → type control → Enter) and go to Devices and Printers. [Adding a network printer]  
2. Click Add a printer. If Windows finds the printer in the list, select it and click Next. [Adding a network printer]  
3. If it isn’t listed, choose The printer that I want isn't listed → Add a printer using a TCP/IP address or hostname. [Adding a network printer]  
4. Select Device type = TCP/IP Device, enter the printer’s IP address, and click Next. Uncheck “Query the printer and automatically select the driver to use” if prompted. [Adding a network printer]  
5. Install the printer driver (select from the list or use Have Disk to point to a downloaded driver). [Adding a network printer]  
6. Name the printer, choose whether to set it as default, click Next, then Finish. Print a test page to confirm. [Adding a network printer]  
7. Verify the printer appears under Devices and Printers and review Printer properties as needed. [Adding a network printer]

Quick troubleshooting if it won’t add or print
- Verify the printer’s network connection and IP is correct.  
- Check firewall/security software isn’t blocking the printer.  
- Restart the printer and your PC.  
- Update or reinstall the printer driver from the manufacturer’s site. [Printer not printing: basic troubleshooting]

Sources:
- Adding a network printer (1f655684-f480-5568-8501-2ddfe150a3a6)  
- Printer not printing: basic troubleshooting (ad317b1e-d2d8-5ce2-8a24-61291d045de2)

If you tell me your OS version (e.g., Windows 10, Windows 11, or macOS) I can give steps specific to that system.
rows.faithfulness_justification: All steps and troubleshooting tips match the provided sources, including prerequisites, Control Panel workflow, TCP/IP method, driver installation, and firewall/driver/restart checks.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Clear, step-by-step Windows instructions with troubleshooting are actionable, but it only covers Windows; users on other OSes would need a follow-up for platform-specific steps.

rows.query: Are we allowed to use AI tools like ChatGPT for work tasks?
rows.answer:
Short answer: Yes — but only under the rules in our AI policy.

Key points from the Acceptable Use of AI Tools Policy:
- Get authorization first: use only AI tools that are pre‑approved by your manager or IT.  
- Use only vetted/sanctioned tools; do not use unapproved SaaS/AI services.  
- Do NOT enter sensitive or confidential data into AI tools unless you have explicit authorization.  
- Follow legal/compliance requirements (GDPR/CCPA etc.) and consult Legal if unsure.  
- Review AI-generated content for accuracy and IP issues before sharing or using it.  
- Keep tools updated, report security incidents, complete required training, and expect monitoring; violations can lead to disciplinary action. [Acceptable Use of AI Tools Policy]

If you want to use a specific tool (e.g., ChatGPT), I can help start an approval request or check whether it’s already sanctioned — would you like me to do that?

Sources:
Acceptable Use of AI Tools Policy (130fb1d5-c526-5bf6-b8c9-d097935bf426)
rows.faithfulness_justification: All points in the answer align with and are supported by the Acceptable Use of AI Tools Policy in the sources (authorization, vetted tools, data privacy, compliance, review, IP, security, training, monitoring, and consequences).
rows.helpfulness_justification: Directly answers ‘yes, with conditions,’ concisely lists the applicable rules, and offers next-step assistance to check or initiate approval for specific tools.

rows.query: What do I need to do for IT setup on my first day?
rows.answer:
Short checklist for your first-day IT setup:

- Before start: make sure a new-user account request is submitted with your full name, job title, department, start date, preferred email, required access level, and any special software needs. [Requesting a New User Account]  
- Approvals: the request needs manager (and HR, if required) approval before IT creates the account. [Requesting a New User Account]  
- Submission & tracking: the request is submitted through the IT portal; you or your manager can monitor its status via the helpdesk ticketing system. [Requesting a New User Account]  
- After account creation: you’ll receive your username, a temporary password, and first-login instructions. Schedule a short session with IT to assist with the initial login. [Requesting a New User Account]  
- First-day setup tasks with IT: set your permanent password, confirm access to email and critical applications, and have required software installed or granted. [Requesting a New User Account]  
- If anything fails or you have questions, contact the IT helpdesk for ongoing support. [Requesting a New User Account]

Sources: Requesting a New User Account (fdc317dc-f5e5-57b4-bf72-3fa985772aa3)
rows.faithfulness_justification: Most points map to the sources, but claims about the manager being able to track the ticket, setting a permanent password, and getting required software installed are not explicitly supported.
rows.helpfulness_justification: Clear, concise checklist that largely answers first-day setup needs with actionable steps, though it omits some details and includes minor assumptions.
